title: Set up drawing-machine branch
description: Starting from the already-prepared repository state, create and switch to branch drawing-machine so implementation can begin in an isolated branch. Done when the working branch is drawing-machine and ready for development handoff.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Repository root /workspace/processing-sketch-book is used. 2. Branch drawing-machine exists locally and is checked out. 3. No implementation work starts before this branch setup is complete.

title: Implement drawing-machine sketch
description: Implement the drawing-machine sketch on branch drawing-machine. The sketch must use three invisible intersecting rotating lines, randomized startup speeds, and a connected drawing trail that follows the changing intersection points over time.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Sketch behavior is driven by three rotating lines that are not visibly rendered as final artwork. 2. Startup speeds are randomized. 3. A connected trail is drawn from successive intersection points. 4. Result matches the requested drawing-machine concept well enough to proceed to validation.

title: Unblock validation with GUI-capable review path
notes: Added SpinningDiamond/GUI_REVIEW.md and SpinningDiamond/run-gui-review.sh to provide a GUI-capable validation path and clear headless fallback messaging. Verified Processing CLI build passes; interactive run remains blocked in this environment due DISPLAY unset.

title: Provision Processing toolchain (processing-java) in workspace
description:
Ensure the processing-java CLI (or equivalent Processing headless runner) is available in the workspace execution environment before any sketch work begins.

Acceptance criteria:
1. processing-java binary is resolvable on PATH (or a documented equivalent such as the Processing application bundle or Docker image with Processing pre-installed).
2. A smoke-test command (e.g., processing-java --sketch=/tmp/test --run) exits without 'command not found'.
3. Approved approach is documented in AGENTS.md or a new TOOLCHAIN.md so subsequent agents/developers know how to reproduce the environment.

Recommended options (pick one and document):
- Option A: Install Processing 4.x tarball and symlink processing-java to /usr/local/bin.
- Option B: Use a Docker image that bundles Processing (e.g., openjdk base + Processing download).
- Option C: Obtain team/infra approval to pre-install Processing on the shared runner/devcontainer via copilot-setup-steps.yml or equivalent.

Done when: processing-java --help (or agreed equivalent) exits 0 in the target execution environment and the chosen approach is recorded.
notes: Provisioned approved equivalent to processing-java: Processing 4.5.2 portable CLI at /workspace/processing-sketch-book/.tools/processing-4.5.2/Processing/bin/Processing. Use command: /workspace/processing-sketch-book/.tools/processing-4.5.2/Processing/bin/Processing cli --sketch=<ABSOLUTE_SKETCH_DIR> --run. Availability verified with: /workspace/processing-sketch-book/.tools/processing-4.5.2/Processing/bin/Processing cli --help (exit 0). Checkout readiness check command: test -x /workspace/processing-sketch-book/.tools/processing-4.5.2/Processing/bin/Processing && /workspace/processing-sketch-book/.tools/processing-4.5.2/Processing/bin/Processing cli --help

title: Add left-mouse-button camera orbit around diamond
description: Extend SpinningDiamond sketch with interactive camera orbit: left mouse button drag orbits the camera around the centered diamond (yaw + pitch). Recommended approach: use PeasyCam library (already available in Processing) with default left-drag orbit, OR implement manual spherical-coordinate camera using mousePressed/mouseDragged and camera(). The diamond's auto-Y-spin must continue independently of orbit state. Done when: dragging left mouse button smoothly orbits the view around the diamond from any angle without breaking the spin animation.
notes: Implemented left-mouse camera orbit in SpinningDiamond/SpinningDiamond.pde. Added spherical camera state (orbitYaw/orbitPitch/orbitRadius) and per-frame camera() targeting the centered diamond at (width*0.5,height*0.5,0). Added mouseDragged handler gated to LEFT button to adjust yaw/pitch with pitch clamping for smooth orbit. Kept existing rotationY self-spin logic intact and independent. Validation: attempted Processing CLI build via .tools/processing-4.5.2/Processing/processing-java --build, but processing-java executable not present in this environment. Interaction note for validation task: run sketch and verify left-drag orbits camera while diamond continues auto-rotating on Y-axis without pausing.

title: Implement 720x720 spinning diamond Processing sketch
description: Create a new Processing sketch folder (e.g., SpinningDiamond/SpinningDiamond.pde) in the repo. Requirements: size(720, 720, P3D); black background; a diamond shape (octahedron geometry) centered in the scene; reflective/specular material using Processing lights() + specular()/shininess(); the diamond auto-rotates continuously on its own Y-axis (rotateY angle incremented each draw()). Use PeasyCam or manual matrix transforms for the 3D geometry. Done when: sketch renders a visually centered, specular diamond spinning on Y-axis at 720x720 with a black background.
notes: Implemented base sketch only (no camera orbit) on branch spinning-diamod. Added file: SpinningDiamond/SpinningDiamond.pde. Sketch uses size(720, 720, P3D), black background, centered octahedron geometry, lights + directionalLight + specular + shininess for reflective look, and continuous self-rotation via rotateY(rotationY) with rotationY increment in draw(). Follow-on camera task (processing-sketch-book-2pf): keep current self-rotation intact and layer left-mouse orbit camera controls on top without changing geometry definition.

title: Create branch spinning-diamod from master
description: From /workspace/processing-sketch-book, run: git checkout master && git pull origin master && git checkout -b spinning-diamod. Push the branch: git push -u origin spinning-diamod. Done when: branch spinning-diamod exists locally and on remote, tracking origin/spinning-diamod.
notes: Branch prep executed in /workspace/processing-sketch-book. Commands and outcomes: (1) git --no-pager fetch origin => success; (2) git checkout master => already on 'master', tracking origin/master; (3) git --no-pager pull --ff-only origin master => already up to date; (4) git checkout -b spinning-diamod => created new local branch; (5) git --no-pager push -u origin spinning-diamod => pushed new remote branch and set upstream. Resulting branch state: current_branch=spinning-diamod; upstream=origin/spinning-diamod; remote_branch_ref=refs/heads/spinning-diamod; git branch -vv: spinning-diamod 35f0ecb [origin/spinning-diamod].

title: Checkout and set up processing-sketch-book repository
description: Clone git@github.com:ValerianWeiss/processing-sketch-book.git into /workspace/processing-sketch-book (already done). Verify the Processing toolchain is available (processing-java or equivalent). Confirm the repo structure and any existing sketches. Done when: repo is cloned at /workspace/processing-sketch-book and toolchain is confirmed usable.
notes:
Setup-readiness check results: repo exists at /workspace/processing-sketch-book on branch master tracking origin/master; remote origin configured as git@github.com:ValerianWeiss/processing-sketch-book.git; remote access check succeeded via git ls-remote --heads origin (master visible). Repository structure currently contains AGENTS.md, .gitignore, .beads/, .git/ with no Processing sketch files detected. Processing CLI toolchain blocker: processing-java command not found in environment, so Processing toolchain is not yet confirmed usable.
Readiness re-check completed after Processing CLI provisioning. Findings: (1) checkout usable at /workspace/processing-sketch-book, git rev-parse confirms work tree=true, branch master tracking origin/master; (2) repo structure verified (AGENTS.md, .gitignore, .beads/, .git/, .tools/processing-4.5.2 plus portable zip); (3) remote configured as git@github.com:ValerianWeiss/processing-sketch-book.git and remote access verified via git ls-remote --heads origin (master: 35f0ecb4cce388047031fbb24344b4e2f4aab3cb); (4) approved toolchain command succeeded exactly: test -x /workspace/processing-sketch-book/.tools/processing-4.5.2/Processing/bin/Processing && /workspace/processing-sketch-book/.tools/processing-4.5.2/Processing/bin/Processing cli --help (exit 0). Blocker removed; setup readiness complete for next branch-creation task.

title: Validate drawing-machine sketch
description: Run the project's existing validation workflow for the drawing-machine sketch and confirm the implemented behavior meets the requested feature expectations before commit.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Validation follows the repository's existing workflow. 2. Validation confirms the drawing-machine behavior and trail output are acceptable. 3. Any blocking defect found during validation is captured before commit proceeds.
notes:
Validation path for rerun/runtime evidence:

Preferred path (matches prior GUI-capable Processing 4 review approach used to unblock sketch validation):
1. Use a machine with a display and Processing 4 installed (4.5.2 or current 4.x is fine).
2. Open /workspace/processing-sketch-book/DrawingMachine/DrawingMachine.pde in Processing 4.
3. Run the sketch and verify the drawing-machine behavior visually.
4. Capture runtime evidence as a short screen recording or screenshots. If rerun from this workspace, use /workspace/processing-sketch-book/DrawingMachine/review.mp4 as the recording target (or save screenshots alongside it).
5. Record pass/fail results against qbl.3 before commit/PR routing resumes.

Documented fallback for this Linux environment if GUI review is unavailable:
- Install prerequisites: Processing 4 CLI/runtime, Java GUI runtime, Xvfb, ffmpeg, and GL libs.
- Example setup/run:
  apt-get update
  apt-get install -y xvfb libxrender1 libxtst6 libxi6 libgl1-mesa-dri libglu1-mesa unzip ffmpeg
  mkdir -p /workspace/processing-sketch-book/.tools /workspace/processing-sketch-book/.tmp-processing-run
  curl -L -o /tmp/processing.zip https://github.com/processing/processing4/releases/download/processing-1313-4.5.2/processing-4.5.2-linux-x64-portable.zip
  unzip -q /tmp/processing.zip -d /workspace/processing-sketch-book/.tools
  Xvfb :99 -screen 0 1280x720x24 >/tmp/xvfb.log 2>&1 &
  export DISPLAY=:99
  ffmpeg -y -video_size 1280x720 -f x11grab -i :99 -t 10 /workspace/processing-sketch-book/DrawingMachine/review.mp4 >/tmp/ffmpeg.log 2>&1 &
  /workspace/processing-sketch-book/.tools/processing-4.5.2/processing --sketch=/workspace/processing-sketch-book/DrawingMachine --output=/workspace/processing-sketch-book/.tmp-processing-run --force --run

Current blocker in this container: no processing/processing-java, java, or xvfb-run installed, so no runtime evidence can be captured here until that setup is added.
Validation (code inspection + compile build) — UI/visual runtime skipped per reviewer instruction.

Build: PASS — Processing 4.5.2 CLI compiled DrawingMachine.pde to .class without errors or warnings.
  Output: .tmp-processing-run/DrawingMachine.class, DrawingMachine$RotatingLine.class

Criteria from qbl.2 (implementation requirements):

1. Three rotating lines not visibly rendered as final artwork: PASS
   - LINE_COUNT = 3; RotatingLine array has exactly 3 elements (lines 1, 6).
   - draw() calls advance() and lineIntersection() for all 3 lines but never calls line()/ellipse()/point()
     on the RotatingLine primitives themselves. The only line() call (line 45) draws the trail between
     intersection-derived points, not the rotating lines.

2. Startup speeds are randomized: PASS
   - randomSignedSpeed() draws from random(MIN_SPEED=0.005, MAX_SPEED=0.02) and randomly negates
     (line 51-54). Each line gets an independent random startAngle and directionOffset/Multiplier
     (lines 21-24). No fixed seeds — results differ per run.

3. Connected trail drawn from successive intersection points: PASS
   - draw() computes all 3 pairwise intersections (lines 35-37), averages them into activePoint
     (line 39), guards on null (line 40), and draws line(previousPoint, activePoint) (line 45).
     previousPoint is updated each frame (line 48). Trail is continuous and accumulates on the
     persistent black background (background(0) in setup only, not in draw).

4. Correct line intersection mathematics: PASS
   - lineIntersection() implements the standard two-line intersection formula using cross-products
     (lines 82-92). Parallel-line guard (abs(denominator) < 0.0001) returns null and is handled
     gracefully by averagePoint(). No division-by-zero risk.

5. Canvas size / sketch structure: PASS
   - size(720, 720) — square canvas, consistent with project conventions (SpinningDiamond also 720x720).
   - Amber stroke (255, 220, 120, 140) with alpha gives a natural fading/overlap aesthetic.
   - smooth(8) applied for anti-aliased trail rendering.

No blocking defects found. Sketch is ready for commit.

title: Open pull request: spinning-diamod → master
description: Open a GitHub pull request from branch spinning-diamod targeting master. Title: 'Add spinning diamond 3D sketch with camera orbit'. Body must include: description of the sketch (720x720, black bg, Y-spin, specular, LMB orbit), how to run it (open SpinningDiamond/SpinningDiamond.pde in Processing IDE). Use: gh pr create --base master --head spinning-diamod --title '...' --body '...'. Done when: PR is open on GitHub and CI/checks pass (if any).

title: Commit spinning diamond sketch to spinning-diamod branch
description: On branch spinning-diamod: stage all new/modified files under SpinningDiamond/. Run: git add SpinningDiamond/ && git commit -m 'Add spinning diamond sketch with camera orbit'. Push: git push origin spinning-diamod. Done when: commit exists on origin/spinning-diamod containing the complete sketch.

title: Validate spinning diamond sketch
description: Run the SpinningDiamond sketch and verify all acceptance criteria: (1) window is 720x720 pixels, (2) background is black, (3) diamond is centered and visible, (4) diamond spins continuously on its Y-axis, (5) reflective/specular highlight is visible, (6) left-mouse drag orbits the camera smoothly. Document any visual defects as child bugs. Done when: all 6 criteria pass with no visual regressions.
notes:
Reviewer validation on branch spinning-diamod:
- Criterion 1 (720x720 window): PASS (size(720, 720, P3D) in SpinningDiamond.pde:11; CLI build succeeds).
- Criterion 2 (black background): PASS (background(0) in draw at line 16).
- Criterion 3 (centered visible diamond): PASS by code inspection (translate(width*0.5, height*0.5, 0) and drawOctahedron(160)); visual visibility not directly observed due headless environment.
- Criterion 4 (continuous Y-axis spin): PASS by code inspection (rotateY(rotationY), rotationY += 0.02 each frame).
- Criterion 5 (visible reflective/specular lighting): FAIL (specular/shininess + lights configured, but visibility of highlight could not be validated because sketch cannot run without display in this environment).
- Criterion 6 (smooth left-mouse camera orbit): FAIL (mouseDragged logic for LEFT button/yaw/pitch exists, but smooth interactive orbit cannot be validated without display/input runtime).
Environment limitation: Processing CLI run fails headlessly with DISPLAY unset: "Cannot run sketch without a display... Target VM failed to initialize."

title: Commit drawing-machine sketch
description: Create the implementation commit for the drawing-machine sketch after validation passes. Done when the finished sketch changes are committed on branch drawing-machine with a clear commit message.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Only validated drawing-machine changes are staged. 2. A commit is created on branch drawing-machine. 3. Commit message clearly describes the sketch addition.

title: Open pull request for drawing-machine sketch
description: If the drawing-machine sketch is completed, open a pull request from branch drawing-machine following the repository's normal PR process. Done when the PR is filed with enough detail for review, or intentionally skipped only because the sketch is not yet complete.
acceptance_criteria: 1. PR is opened only if the sketch is completed. 2. PR clearly summarizes the drawing-machine sketch and validation outcome. 3. Target branch and source branch are correct for repository workflow.

title: Create drawing-machine sketch feature
description: Deliver the drawing-machine sketch with an execution-ready implementation path. Completed setup prerequisites are already satisfied: repository cloned, .beads confirmed, and local Beads DB restored via bd bootstrap. Use this feature to route execution through branch setup, sketch implementation, validation, commit creation, and PR filing.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Execution plan explicitly records completed setup prerequisites. 2. Work proceeds on branch drawing-machine. 3. Sketch implementation produces three invisible intersecting rotating lines with randomized startup speeds and a connected trail drawn through their intersection points. 4. Validation uses the project's existing workflow. 5. Work is committed once validated. 6. PR is filed if the sketch is completed.
